lesson.title: Hypothesis Testing
lesson.body: Hypothesis testing is a formal procedure for deciding between a null hypothesis H₀ and an alternative H₁ based on data. We compute a test statistic and reject H₀ if it falls in the rejection region. A Type I error (false positive) occurs with probability α. A Type II error (false negative) occurs with probability β. The p-value is the probability of observing results as extreme as the data, assuming H₀ is true.

rooms.question: A Type I error is:
answers: Rejecting H₀ when H₀ is true
Failing to reject H₀ when H₁ is true
rooms.explanation: Type I error = false positive = rejecting a true null hypothesis. Its probability is controlled by the significance level α.

rooms.question: For a two-sided z-test at α = 0.05, we reject H₀: μ = μ₀ if:
rooms.explanation: Two-sided test at α=0.05 rejects when |Z| > z_{0.025} = 1.96.

rooms.question: What does a p-value of 0.03 mean?
answers: Under H₀, there is a 3% probability of observing results as extreme as those obtained
There is a 3% chance of a Type I error in this specific test
rooms.explanation: The p-value is a conditional probability: P(data as extreme | H₀ true). A common misconception is that it is P(H₀ is true | data).

rooms.question: Order the steps in a hypothesis test:
items: State H₀ and H₁, choose α
Compute the test statistic from data
Find the p-value or critical region
Reject or fail to reject H₀
rooms.explanation: Proper order: define hypotheses and significance level first, then compute, then decide.

rooms.question: Power of a test = 1 − β is defined as:
rooms.explanation: Power = P(correctly rejecting H₀ when H₁ is true) = 1 − β (Type II error rate). Higher power is better.

boss.name: The Null Hypothesis
boss.taunt: You cannot prove I am wrong. You can only find weak evidence against me.

questions.question: When we 'fail to reject H₀', this means:
answers: The data do not provide sufficient evidence against H₀
The test is inconclusive and we accept H₁
Our sample size was too large
questions.explanation: Failing to reject ≠ accepting. We never 'accept' H₀ in classical testing — we simply lack enough evidence to reject it.

questions.question: The t-statistic for testing H₀: μ = μ₀ with unknown σ is:
questions.explanation: With unknown σ, replace it by the sample S and use the t-distribution with n−1 degrees of freedom.

questions.question: The Neyman-Pearson lemma states that the most powerful test at level α for a simple H₀ vs simple H₁ is based on:
questions.explanation: The Neyman-Pearson lemma guarantees that the likelihood ratio test is the most powerful for simple hypotheses, providing the theoretical foundation for many common tests.
